episode_summary: The team discuss self-sowing wild oats and oat lore, China’s rise to electric vehicles including a brand amusingly rendered as “wrong way,” why WWII bombs sometimes whistled due to fitted whistles and broader uses of sound in warfare, and the remarkable regeneration and odd biology of flatworms. Along the way they detour into cereals, cars, bomb disposal heroics, and peculiar parasite traps.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that wild oats sow themselves.

Okay, my fact this week is that the world's first electric internet connected SUV car, which was designed to help you easily locate parking spots and petrol stations was Chinese and called the wrong way.
My fact this week is that the reason a World War II bomb whistled as it fell from the sky is because it had a big whistle on it.
My fact is flatworms sometimes grow heads at both ends by mistake.
